lesson.title: Starting a Business in South Africa
lesson.explanation: Register via CIPC (Companies and Intellectual Property Commission): private company (Pty Ltd) costs R175 online. Steps: 1) Reserve company name, 2) File CoR14.1 (Memorandum of Incorporation), 3) Get registration certificate. Then: register for tax (SARS: income tax, VAT if turnover >R1M), register for UIF (Unemployment Insurance Fund), register for COIDA (workmen's compensation). Municipal business licence may be required. BEE compliance: submit B-BBEE certificate for government tenders. Open business bank account (FNB, Standard Bank, Nedbank, ABSA all offer business accounts).

misconceptions.misconception: You don't need formal training for Starting a Business in South Africa
misconceptions.correction: Proper training and certification are required for safety and legal compliance in SA.
misconceptions.why_students_think_this: People see informal work and assume no qualifications are needed.
